python.name: Python
python.description: Learn Python from basics to problem solving.

python-basics.name: Python Basics
python-basics.description: Start with syntax, variables, and control flow.

python-vars-note.title: Python Variables
python-vars-note.content:
Python variables are created when you assign a value.

Examples:
name = 'CodeSpire'
age = 2
active = True

loops.name: Loops
loops.description: Practice for loops and while loops.

python-loops-note.title: Python Loops
python-loops-note.content:
Use for loops to iterate over ranges and lists.

Example:
for i in range(5):
    print(i)

python-functions.name: Functions & Modules
python-functions.description: Learn to write reusable code with functions and modules.

functions.name: Defining Functions
functions.description: Create and call functions with parameters and return values.

python-func-note.title: Python Functions
python-func-note.content:
Functions are defined using the def keyword.

Example:
def greet(name):
    return f'Hello, {name}!'

print(greet('CodeSpire'))

python-modules-note.title: Python Modules
python-modules-note.content:
Import modules with the import statement.

Example:
import math
print(math.sqrt(16))  # 4.0

from random import randint
print(randint(1, 10))

c-programming.name: C Programming
c-programming.description: Master syntax, memory basics, and structured programs in C.

c-foundations.name: C Foundations
c-foundations.description: Understand the building blocks of C.

c-stdio-note.title: stdio.h Basics

variables-c.name: Variables in C
variables-c.description: Declare and use variables with types.

c-vars-note.title: C Variables

if-else.name: If-Else Statements
if-else.description: Make decisions in your C programs.

c-ifelse-note.title: If-Else in C

sql.name: SQL
sql.description: Query, analyze, and organize data using SQL.

sql-core.name: SQL Core
sql-core.description: Learn SELECT, WHERE, ORDER BY, and aggregates.

select.name: SELECT Queries
select.description: Retrieve data from tables using SELECT.

sql-select-note.title: Basic SELECT

sql-joins-note.title: SQL JOINs
sql-joins-note.content:
JOIN combines rows from two or more tables.

Example:
SELECT s.name, c.course_name
FROM students s
INNER JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.id;

subqueries.name: Subqueries
subqueries.description: Nest queries for complex data retrieval.

sql-subquery-note.title: SQL Subqueries
sql-subquery-note.content:
A subquery is a query nested inside another query.

Example:
SELECT name FROM students
WHERE grade > (SELECT AVG(grade) FROM students);

questions.question: Which keyword is used for a loop over a range in Python?
questions.explanation: Python uses the for keyword to iterate over ranges, lists, and other iterables.

questions.question: What is the output of print(type(3.14))?
questions.explanation: 3.14 is a floating-point number, so type() returns <class 'float'>.

questions.question: Which function is used to get user input in Python?
questions.explanation: The input() function reads a line of text from the user.

questions.question: Which clause filters rows in SQL?
questions.explanation: WHERE is used to filter rows before grouping or ordering.

questions.question: Which JOIN returns all rows from the left table?
questions.explanation: LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right.

questions.question: What does COUNT(*) do?
questions.explanation: COUNT(*) counts the total number of rows in a result set.

questions.question: Which header file is needed for printf()?
questions.explanation: printf() is declared in stdio.h (standard input/output header).

questions.question: What is the size of int on most 64-bit systems?
questions.explanation: On most modern 64-bit systems, int is 4 bytes (32 bits).
